test_name: One-time historical backfill of alert data into consolidated table
test_description: Validate that during initial load, all historical alert data (active alert + alert_archive) is extracted from Moogsoft SQL Server and consolidated into MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD> table in the EDL with alert_id as the primary key.
preconditions: Moogsoft SQL Server source is accessible; alert and alert_archive tables contain historical records; EDL Landing and Raw layers are provisioned; SPN credentials are configured.
Expected Result: Backfill pipeline successfully copies all records older than 13 months from alert_archive and active records (<13 months) from alert into MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD>; alert_id is unique and used as pk_id; record count matches source total.
steps: Step 1: Verify alert and alert_archive source tables are populated and accessible via SPN.
Step 2: Trigger the one-time historical backfill pipeline for alerts.
Step 3: Validate target table MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD> is created with correct cutoff date token.
Step 4: Compare row counts between source (alert + alert_archive) and target consolidated table.
Step 5: Confirm alert_id is the pk_id and contains no duplicates or nulls.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 In Scope - One-Time Historical Backfill; Section 1.5 Functional Requirements - Historical Backfill

test_name: One-time historical backfill of situation data into consolidated table
test_description: Validate that during initial load, all historical situation data (active situation + situation_archive) is consolidated into MLF_AIOps_Situation_Until<YYYYMMDD> with situation_id as the primary key.
preconditions: situation and situation_archive tables exist in Moogsoft SQL Server; EDL is configured; SPN access is granted.
Expected Result: MLF_AIOps_Situation_Until<YYYYMMDD> contains the full union of active and archive situation data; situation_id is pk_id, unique and non-null; row counts reconcile with source.
steps: Step 1: Verify situation and situation_archive source availability.
Step 2: Execute the historical backfill pipeline for situations.
Step 3: Validate the consolidated target table MLF_AIOps_Situation_Until<YYYYMMDD> is created.
Step 4: Reconcile record counts between source and target.
Step 5: Validate uniqueness and non-nullness of situation_id.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 In Scope; Section 1.5 Historical Backfill & Dataset Unification

test_name: Historical consolidated datasets are not re-ingested after backfill
test_description: Validate that once the one-time historical backfill is completed, the MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD> and MLF_AIOps_Situation_Until<YYYYMMDD> tables are not refreshed or re-ingested by any subsequent pipeline run.
preconditions: Historical backfill has been completed successfully; consolidated Until<YYYYMMDD> tables exist.
Expected Result: No scheduled or NRT pipeline modifies, refreshes, or reloads the historical consolidated tables; row counts and content remain immutable across subsequent runs.
steps: Step 1: Capture row count and checksum of the consolidated historical tables post-backfill.
Step 2: Run NRT and daily batch pipelines for multiple cycles.
Step 3: Re-check the row count and checksum of the historical tables.
Step 4: Confirm no modifications occurred.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 - 'These consolidated historical datasets will not be refreshed or re-ingested after the initial load'

test_name: Cutoff date token <YYYYMMDD> applied correctly in table name
test_description: Verify that the <YYYYMMDD> token used in the consolidated historical table name accurately reflects the cutoff date applied to the backfill snapshot.
preconditions: Backfill snapshot cutoff date is defined and documented.
Expected Result: Both MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD> and MLF_AIOps_Situation_Until<YYYYMMDD> table names contain a valid YYYYMMDD date matching the configured snapshot cutoff date.
steps: Step 1: Configure backfill cutoff date.
Step 2: Run backfill pipeline.
Step 3: Inspect target table names in EDL.
Step 4: Validate format YYYYMMDD and value matches configured cutoff.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 In Scope; Section 1.5 Historical Backfill

test_name: Daily batch ingestion of qa_teams (DEV) executes at 9 AM EST
test_description: Verify that daily batch ingestion of qa_teams runs incrementally and is triggered at 9 AM EST, loading new and updated records only into the Landing and Raw layers.
preconditions: qa_teams table populated with watermark column; scheduler configured for 9 AM EST; SPN access available.
Expected Result: Pipeline triggers at 9 AM EST, loads only delta records based on the watermark, and updates Landing and Raw layers without reloading history.
steps: Step 1: Insert and update qa_teams records in source.
Step 2: Wait for or trigger scheduled job at 9 AM EST.
Step 3: Confirm only new/updated records are present in Landing and Raw.
Step 4: Verify watermark column value is advanced after the run.

test_name: Daily batch incremental ingestion for qa_mtta, qa_mttd, qa_mttr
test_description: Validate that qa_mtta, qa_mttd, and qa_mttr datasets are ingested incrementally each day using watermarking, capturing only new and changed records.
preconditions: Source tables qa_mtta/qa_mttd/qa_mttr have watermark columns and incremental records present.
Expected Result: Only delta rows after the previous watermark value are ingested into Raw layer; unchanged rows are skipped; watermark is advanced atomically.
steps: Step 1: Capture current watermark for each table.
Step 2: Insert/update records with timestamps greater than the watermark.
Step 3: Trigger daily batch pipeline.
Step 4: Validate only new/updated rows appear in Raw.
Step 5: Confirm watermark advanced and persisted.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 - Daily Batch Ingestion; Section 1.5 Incremental & Full Load Support

test_name: Daily full load ingestion for onboardedTeams
test_description: Validate that the onboardedTeams table is ingested using a full-load (daily-full) strategy, replacing the target dataset entirely on each run rather than incrementally.
preconditions: onboardedTeams source table populated; pipeline configured as full-load.
Expected Result: Each daily run overwrites/refreshes the onboardedTeams Raw layer dataset with the complete current source content; deleted source rows are not retained in target.
steps: Step 1: Capture current source row count for onboardedTeams.
Step 2: Delete and insert records in source to vary content.
Step 3: Trigger daily batch pipeline.
Step 4: Validate Raw layer matches source exactly post-load.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 - onboardedTeams table will be daily-full load

test_name: NRT ingestion of alert data into MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming
test_description: Validate that the dedicated NRT pipeline ingests new alert events from Event Hub into MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming approximately every 5 seconds using watermark tracking.
preconditions: Event Hub topic moogsoft-alerts-eh is active; SPN gf-etsolive-dev/qa is configured; NRT pipeline is deployed and running.
Expected Result: New alert records are written to MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming within an approximate 5-second ingestion cycle; only inserts and updates are processed; no historical loads occur.
steps: Step 1: Publish test alert events to Event Hub topic.
Step 2: Observe MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming for new rows.
Step 3: Validate latency from event publish to availability is ~5 seconds per cycle.
Step 4: Confirm no archive/history rows are pulled in.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 NRT/Streaming Ingestion; Section 1.5 NRT Ingestion

test_name: NRT ingestion of situation data into MLF_AIOps_Situation_Streaming
test_description: Validate that the NRT pipeline writes situation events from Event Hub to MLF_AIOps_Situation_Streaming using watermark-based capture for inserts and updates only.
preconditions: Event Hub for situations is active; NRT pipeline deployed; watermark column available.
Expected Result: Situation events appear in MLF_AIOps_Situation_Streaming with near real-time latency; inserts and updates handled; no backfill executed.
steps: Step 1: Emit insert and update situation events.
Step 2: Verify both event types are reflected in MLF_AIOps_Situation_Streaming.
Step 3: Confirm watermark advanced for each cycle.
Step 4: Verify no historical/archive data was loaded.

test_name: NRT end-to-end latency within 60 seconds
test_description: Validate that the NRT pipeline delivers alert and situation data to the Raw layer and downstream consumers (e.g., Power BI) within the required ≤60-second end-to-end latency.
preconditions: NRT pipeline running; downstream consumer (Power BI) connected; clock-synchronized test instrumentation.
Expected Result: Time from source event generation to downstream availability is consistently ≤60 seconds across multiple measurements.
steps: Step 1: Emit timestamped test event at source.
Step 2: Measure arrival time in Raw layer.
Step 3: Measure arrival time at Power BI downstream.
Step 4: Confirm total latency ≤60 seconds across ≥30 samples.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Downstream Data Availability; Section 1.6 Performance & Latency

test_name: NRT pipeline runs independently from backfill and batch pipelines
test_description: Validate that the NRT pipeline operates on a separate, dedicated infrastructure and is not impacted by the historical backfill or daily batch ingestion pipelines.
preconditions: Backfill, daily batch, and NRT pipelines are all deployed.
Expected Result: Failures, restarts, or executions of the backfill/batch pipelines do not interrupt or affect NRT ingestion latency or throughput.
steps: Step 1: Start NRT pipeline and observe baseline latency.
Step 2: Trigger daily batch and a re-run of backfill (in lower env).
Step 3: Stop/restart the batch pipeline mid-run.
Step 4: Verify NRT pipeline continues without disruption and remains within latency SLA.

test_name: Logical and physical separation of historical and streaming datasets
test_description: Validate that *_Until<YYYYMMDD> historical and *_Streaming NRT datasets are kept logically and physically separate with no automated unification.
preconditions: Both historical and streaming datasets exist in EDL.
Expected Result: Datasets reside in distinct tables/locations; no pipeline merges them; cross-dataset queries only succeed via explicit JOIN views.
steps: Step 1: Inspect EDL catalog for both dataset families.
Step 2: Confirm distinct storage paths/tables.
Step 3: Verify no ingestion job writes into both.
Step 4: Validate JOIN view between MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD> and MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming returns expected unified result.

test_name: Joined view of historical and streaming datasets for pre-cutoff queries
test_description: Validate that queries needing data both before and after the <YYYYMMDD> cutoff return correct results when issued against the JOIN of historical and streaming tables.
preconditions: Both historical and streaming alert tables are populated with overlapping/contiguous data ranges.
Expected Result: JOIN(MLF_AIOps_Alert_Until<YYYYMMDD>, MLF_AIOps_Alert_Streaming) returns complete dataset spanning historical and streaming periods without duplicates or gaps at the cutoff boundary.
steps: Step 1: Identify alerts spanning before and after the cutoff date.
Step 2: Query joined view.
Step 3: Validate row counts and absence of duplicates at the boundary.
Step 4: Confirm chronological continuity.

test_name: Schema conformity and data type validation before Raw load
test_description: Validate that the system enforces schema conformity, expected data types, and presence of required fields prior to loading data into the Raw layer.
preconditions: Schema definitions are configured; test data includes valid and schema-violating records.
Expected Result: Records that match schema/data type rules pass validation and are loaded; records that violate schema are rejected/quarantined with logged validation failures.
steps: Step 1: Inject mix of valid and invalid records (wrong types, missing required fields).
Step 2: Run ingestion pipeline.
Step 3: Verify valid records reach Raw and invalid records are rejected/quarantined.
Step 4: Confirm validation failures appear in logs.

test_name: No PII or PHI fields ingested
test_description: Validate that no PII or PHI fields from Moogsoft data are extracted, stored, or transmitted into the Landing or Raw EDL layers.
preconditions: PII/PHI exclusion policy and field denylist are configured; data scanning rules in place.
Expected Result: Scans of Landing and Raw datasets show no PII or PHI attributes; any detection triggers immediate alert and governance notification.
steps: Step 1: Run automated PII/PHI scanner across Landing and Raw datasets.
Step 2: Verify scan results show zero violations.
Step 3: Inject a synthetic PII record into source.
Step 4: Confirm record is blocked/quarantined and governance alert is generated.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.4 Constraints - Data Privacy; Section 1.5 Data Quality & Validation; Section 1.6 Compliance & Governance

test_name: Malformed records are rejected and logged
test_description: Validate that invalid or malformed records are rejected by the ingestion pipeline and the rejection details are written to logs for remediation.
preconditions: Logging and quarantine destinations configured.
Expected Result: Malformed records do not enter Raw layer; rejection logs contain timestamp, record identifier (or hash), and reason for rejection.
steps: Step 1: Inject malformed JSON/row into source or Event Hub.
Step 2: Run pipeline.
Step 3: Validate record is not present in Raw layer.
Step 4: Verify log/quarantine entry created with descriptive failure reason.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Data Quality & Validation; Section 1.6 Data Quality

test_name: SCD Type 2 versioning using source last_state_change
test_description: Validate that NRT ingestion implements system-driven SCD Type 2 versioning by closing the previous version (End_AT = last_state_change) and inserting a new version (Start_AT = last_state_change) when a newer event for the same key arrives.
preconditions: NRT pipeline configured with SCD2 logic; sample records with multiple last_state_change updates available.
Expected Result: For every update to an existing key, prior row's End_AT equals new event's last_state_change and a new row is inserted with Start_AT = last_state_change; previous open versions are properly closed; only one current/open row per key exists.
steps: Step 1: Insert original alert event v1 with last_state_change=T1.
Step 2: Emit updated event v2 with last_state_change=T2.
Step 3: Query SCD2 target table by alert key.
Step 4: Verify v1 row End_AT=T2 and v2 row Start_AT=T2 with End_AT null/open.
Step 5: Repeat with a third update and confirm only the latest version is open.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Addendum - System-Driven SCD Type 2 Versioning

test_name: System-generated insertdt represents ingestion time
test_description: Validate that the system populates the insertdt field with the actual ingestion timestamp for every NRT record, since this field is not provided by the source.
preconditions: NRT pipeline generates insertdt during write.
Expected Result: Every ingested record contains a non-null insertdt value reflecting the ingestion time (within a few seconds of pipeline write); value is monotonically reasonable across batches.
steps: Step 1: Emit a timestamped event to Event Hub.
Step 2: Capture ingestion time on the pipeline side.
Step 3: Query target table for insertdt of that record.
Step 4: Confirm insertdt aligns with ingestion clock and is non-null.

test_name: System-generated db_alert_id and db_situation_id are unique
test_description: Validate that the ingestion system generates db_alert_id for alert records and db_situation_id for situation records, since the source does not provide these identifiers, and that the generated values are unique.
preconditions: NRT pipeline configured to generate ids; sample alert and situation events available.
Expected Result: Every alert row has a non-null unique db_alert_id; every situation row has a non-null unique db_situation_id; no collisions across the dataset.
steps: Step 1: Emit batches of alert and situation events.
Step 2: Query streaming tables for the generated id columns.
Step 3: Verify uniqueness via count(distinct)=count(*).
Step 4: Verify no null values exist.

test_name: SPN-based authentication enforced for ingestion
test_description: Validate that all ingestion pipelines authenticate using approved Service Principals (e.g., gf-etsolive-dev, gf-etsolive-qa) with least-privilege access, and pipelines fail when SPN credentials are invalid.
preconditions: SPNs registered and access-bound; ability to substitute invalid credentials in test.
Expected Result: Pipelines run successfully with approved SPN; runs with invalid/expired/unauthorized SPN fail authentication and produce clear error messages without writing data.
steps: Step 1: Run pipeline with approved SPN and verify success.
Step 2: Substitute SPN with an unauthorized identity.
Step 3: Trigger pipeline.
Step 4: Verify authentication failure, no data written, and security log entry.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.4 Constraints - Security & Access Control; Section 1.5 Security & Controlled Access

test_name: Role-based access to Raw layer datasets
test_description: Validate that access to ingested Moogsoft datasets in Landing and Raw layers is governed by platform-approved security roles and that unauthorized users cannot read or write to the datasets.
preconditions: RBAC roles configured; test users provisioned with authorized and unauthorized roles.
Expected Result: Authorized users/services can query datasets per their role permissions; unauthorized users receive an access denied error and the attempt is logged.
steps: Step 1: Authenticate as authorized user and query dataset.
Step 2: Authenticate as unauthorized user and attempt the same query.
Step 3: Verify the second attempt is denied.
Step 4: Confirm denial audit log entry.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Security & Controlled Access; Section 1.6 Security

test_name: Encryption in transit and at rest
test_description: Validate that any sensitive data is encrypted in transit between Moogsoft/Event Hub and EDL and at rest in Landing and Raw layers per enterprise security policy.
preconditions: TLS configurations and storage encryption keys provisioned.
Expected Result: Network traffic uses TLS 1.2+ and storage is encrypted with approved keys; encryption can be evidenced via configuration inspection.
steps: Step 1: Capture network handshake between pipeline and source/Event Hub.
Step 2: Verify TLS protocol/version is approved.
Step 3: Inspect storage account/container encryption settings.
Step 4: Confirm encryption is enabled with approved key management.

test_name: Ingestion activity logging completeness
test_description: Validate that the system logs all ingestion activities including extraction, validation, and load events with timestamps, error descriptions, and run metadata.
preconditions: Logging sinks configured and retention policy set.
Expected Result: Every pipeline run emits logs covering each stage; logs contain timestamps, run id, dataset name, row counts, status, and error details when applicable.
steps: Step 1: Execute a successful pipeline run.
Step 2: Execute a failing pipeline run.
Step 3: Inspect logs for both runs.
Step 4: Validate all required fields present and stages covered.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Monitoring, Logging, and Error Handling; Section 1.6 Observability & Monitoring

test_name: Alerts generated on ingestion failures and latency breaches
test_description: Validate that the system generates alerts when ingestion fails, schema mismatches occur, NRT latency exceeds 60 seconds, or data quality errors are detected.
preconditions: Alerting integration (e.g., monitoring tool, email/Teams) configured.
Expected Result: Each failure scenario triggers an alert to the configured channel with descriptive context; no alert is generated for healthy runs.
steps: Step 1: Simulate pipeline failure (e.g., kill task).
Step 2: Inject schema mismatch.
Step 3: Throttle NRT to exceed 60-second latency.
Step 4: Verify alerts are raised for each scenario with appropriate severity and details.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Monitoring & Error Handling; Section 1.6 Observability & Monitoring

test_name: Automatic recovery from transient failures
test_description: Validate that the ingestion pipeline automatically recovers from transient failures (e.g., temporary network blip, source throttling) and resumes ingestion without manual intervention or data loss.
preconditions: Retry logic configured; ability to simulate transient errors.
Expected Result: Pipeline retries automatically per policy, recovers without manual action, and processes all records without duplication or loss after recovery.
steps: Step 1: Inject a transient network error during a run.
Step 2: Observe retry attempts in logs.
Step 3: Verify pipeline completes successfully.
Step 4: Reconcile source and target counts to confirm zero data loss or duplication.

test_name: Run metadata captured for every ingestion execution
test_description: Validate that every NRT and batch ingestion run records reliable run-time metadata including success/failure status, start/end time, row counts, and watermark progression.
preconditions: Metadata table or run history store enabled.
Expected Result: Each run is registered with a unique run id and complete metadata; metadata accurately reflects observable outcomes.
steps: Step 1: Trigger an NRT and a batch run.
Step 2: Query the run metadata store.
Step 3: Validate all required fields are populated and accurate.

test_name: Ingestion handles ~12-13 GB current dataset volume
test_description: Validate that the solution successfully ingests the current full Moogsoft dataset volume (~12-13 GB) during the first historical backfill run without timeouts or resource exhaustion.
preconditions: Production-scale data available in lower environment for load test.
Expected Result: Backfill completes within acceptable runtime window; no out-of-memory, no source/sink throttling-related failures; full fidelity preserved.
steps: Step 1: Stage 12-13 GB representative dataset.
Step 2: Execute backfill pipeline end-to-end.
Step 3: Monitor resource utilization, throughput, and runtime.
Step 4: Validate all source rows are loaded with full fidelity.

test_name: Source system performance protection during ingestion
test_description: Validate that the ingestion process does not degrade Moogsoft SQL Server performance or availability under normal and peak loads.
preconditions: Source DB monitoring instrumented; baseline performance metrics captured.
Expected Result: Source CPU, memory, IO, and query latency remain within acceptable bounds during ingestion; no production incidents triggered.
steps: Step 1: Capture baseline source metrics.
Step 2: Run backfill and concurrent batch jobs.
Step 3: Capture metrics during ingestion.
Step 4: Compare and confirm deltas are within acceptable thresholds.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.4 Constraints - Source System Performance Protection

test_name: Daily batch completes within operational window
test_description: Validate that all daily batch-refreshed Moogsoft datasets complete the daily refresh within the defined operational window starting at 9 AM EST.
preconditions: Daily batch schedule configured; SLA window defined.
Expected Result: All five datasets (qa_teams, qa_mtta, qa_mttd, qa_mttr, onboardedTeams) complete refresh within the SLA window; SLA breaches generate alerts.
steps: Step 1: Trigger 9 AM EST daily batch.
Step 2: Track start and end time of each dataset's pipeline.
Step 3: Verify all complete within SLA.
Step 4: Simulate slow run and verify SLA-breach alert.

test_name: Watermark-based incremental capture skips already-loaded records
test_description: Validate that the watermark mechanism correctly captures only records newer than the last successful run's watermark, preventing duplicate ingestion.
preconditions: Watermark column populated; previous successful run completed.
Expected Result: Records with watermark <= last run's high-watermark are skipped; only newer records are loaded; no duplicates appear in target.
steps: Step 1: Capture last run watermark.
Step 2: Insert mix of old (<= watermark) and new (> watermark) records.
Step 3: Run pipeline.
Step 4: Validate only new records are loaded and no duplicates exist.

test_name: Pipeline behavior when watermark column is missing or null
test_description: Validate that the pipeline handles missing or null watermark column values gracefully by failing safely with a clear error rather than producing incorrect deltas.
preconditions: Test source contains records with null watermark or missing watermark column.
Expected Result: Pipeline detects the issue, fails fast with a descriptive error, raises an alert, and does not load partial/incorrect data.
steps: Step 1: Inject record with null watermark value.
Step 2: Remove watermark column from a test source.
Step 3: Run pipeline.
Step 4: Verify clear failure, alert, and no corrupt data in target.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Data Quality & Validation; Incremental Load Support

test_name: Source SQL Server unavailable during batch run
test_description: Validate behavior when Moogsoft SQL Server is unavailable or unreachable at the time of a scheduled batch run.
preconditions: Ability to simulate SQL Server outage or block network access.
Expected Result: Pipeline retries per policy and fails gracefully with alert; no partial data written to Raw; next successful run resumes correctly from the prior watermark.
steps: Step 1: Block connectivity to SQL Server.
Step 2: Trigger batch run.
Step 3: Observe retries and final failure.
Step 4: Restore connectivity and trigger next run; validate clean resume.

test_name: Event Hub disconnect during NRT ingestion
test_description: Validate that the NRT pipeline handles Event Hub connectivity loss gracefully and resumes from the last committed offset/watermark upon reconnection.
preconditions: Ability to simulate Event Hub outage.
Expected Result: Pipeline pauses or retries during outage, recovers automatically when Event Hub is reachable, and processes all queued events without data loss or duplication.
steps: Step 1: Establish baseline NRT ingestion.
Step 2: Simulate Event Hub disconnect.
Step 3: Restore connectivity after a defined period.
Step 4: Validate all events emitted during outage are eventually ingested with no duplicates.

test_name: 13-month boundary cutoff between active and archive tables
test_description: Validate the boundary handling between active (alert/situation) tables (<13 months) and *_archive tables (>13 months) during backfill so that no record is missed or double-counted at the boundary.
preconditions: Source contains records aged exactly 13 months and on either side of the boundary.
Expected Result: Each record appears exactly once in the consolidated MLF_AIOps_*_Until<YYYYMMDD> table; boundary records are not duplicated or dropped.
steps: Step 1: Seed records exactly at and around the 13-month boundary.
Step 2: Run backfill.
Step 3: Query consolidated table and validate single occurrence of each boundary record.
Step 4: Reconcile total counts against source union.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 In Scope - One-Time Historical Backfill

test_name: Daily batch with no source changes results in zero new rows
test_description: Validate that when there are no new or updated records since the last batch run, the incremental pipeline completes successfully without loading any data and without errors.
preconditions: No source changes between two consecutive runs.
Expected Result: Pipeline runs successfully, loads 0 rows, advances or retains watermark appropriately, and logs a clear 'no new records' message.
steps: Step 1: Complete a successful batch run.
Step 2: Make no changes to source data.
Step 3: Trigger next scheduled run.
Step 4: Verify zero rows loaded and clean run status.

test_name: Schema drift detection at source
test_description: Validate that when the Moogsoft source schema changes unexpectedly (column added/removed/type changed), the pipeline detects the drift, raises an alert, and prevents corrupting Raw data.
preconditions: Ability to modify schema in lower environment.
Expected Result: Pipeline detects schema mismatch, halts loading or quarantines affected records, raises alert with details, and Raw table remains consistent.
steps: Step 1: Add an unexpected column to a source table.
Step 2: Run pipeline.
Step 3: Verify drift is detected and handled per policy.
Step 4: Remove a required column and verify pipeline fails safely with alert.

test_name: Configuration-driven onboarding of a new Moogsoft table
test_description: Validate that a new Moogsoft table can be onboarded into the ingestion framework via configuration changes only, without significant code changes.
preconditions: Framework supports configuration-driven onboarding; sample new table available.
Expected Result: New table is ingested successfully after configuration update; no code deployment required beyond the configuration change.
steps: Step 1: Add new table definition to pipeline configuration.
Step 2: Deploy configuration update.
Step 3: Trigger pipeline.
Step 4: Verify new table data appears correctly in Landing and Raw layers.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.5 Extensibility & Maintainability; Section 1.6 Maintainability & Extensibility

test_name: Modification of ingestion schedule without redesign
test_description: Validate that batch ingestion schedules can be modified (e.g., change 9 AM EST to a different time) through configuration without major redesign or downtime.
preconditions: Scheduler exposes configuration; change management approved.
Expected Result: New schedule is applied without breaking pipelines; pipelines run at new time on next cycle; previous schedule no longer triggers.
steps: Step 1: Update schedule configuration to a new time.
Step 2: Deploy and validate scheduler.
Step 3: Confirm runs occur at new time and not at old time.

test_name: DEV vs PROD environment dataset naming and connectivity
test_description: Validate that DEV and PROD pipelines connect to correct SQL Server endpoints and use correct table names (qa_* vs prod_*) per environment.
preconditions: Environment-specific configurations deployed.
Expected Result: DEV pipeline connects to esmretscacmoogsoft-sql.database.windows.net and reads qa_teams/qa_mtta/qa_mttd/qa_mttr; PROD pipeline connects to esmreprcacmoogsoft-sql.database.windows.net and reads prod_teams/prod_mtta/prod_mttd/prod_mttr; no cross-environment access occurs.
steps: Step 1: Run DEV pipeline and inspect connection logs and table names.
Step 2: Run PROD pipeline and inspect connection logs and table names.
Step 3: Verify each environment hits only its intended endpoint and tables.
requirement_mapping: Section 2.2 Source Connection Details; Section 2.3 Table names

test_name: Event Hub consumer group and SPN configuration
test_description: Validate that NRT ingestion uses the correct Event Hub namespace, server, topic (moogsoft-alerts-eh), $Default consumer group, and environment-specific SPN (gf-etsolive-dev for DEV, gf-etsolive-qa for QA).
preconditions: Event Hub and SPN credentials provisioned per environment.
Expected Result: Pipeline connects with the correct namespace/server/topic/consumer and SPN per environment; misconfiguration causes a clear connection error.
steps: Step 1: Run NRT pipeline in DEV; verify connection params match esmretscaemoogsoftevnthb-ns and gf-etsolive-dev SPN.
Step 2: Run NRT pipeline in QA; verify gf-etsolive-qa SPN is used.
Step 3: Misconfigure consumer group; verify connection failure with clear error.

test_name: Raw layer datasets exposed to downstream Power BI
test_description: Validate that Raw-layer Moogsoft datasets are accessible to downstream consumers such as Power BI, subject to access policies.
preconditions: Power BI workspace configured; access roles applied.
Expected Result: Authorized Power BI users can query and visualize Raw layer datasets; unauthorized users are denied; data freshness matches ingestion cadence.
steps: Step 1: Connect Power BI to Raw layer datasets.
Step 2: Verify dataset visibility for authorized user.
Step 3: Confirm unauthorized user is blocked.
Step 4: Validate data freshness reflects latest ingestion.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.3 Assumptions - Downstream Access; Section 1.5 Downstream Data Availability

test_name: Data lineage and audit trail maintained
test_description: Validate that complete data lineage and audit trails are maintained for all ingestion activities to satisfy governance and compliance requirements.
preconditions: Lineage/audit tooling integrated with pipeline.
Expected Result: Lineage shows source table -> Landing -> Raw mappings; audit logs capture who/what/when for every ingestion run; logs are retained per policy.
steps: Step 1: Execute a pipeline run.
Step 2: Inspect lineage tooling for end-to-end mapping.
Step 3: Verify audit log contains run identity, timestamps, datasets, and outcomes.
Step 4: Confirm retention policy compliance.

test_name: Out-of-scope curated/semantic transformation is not performed
test_description: Validate that the ingestion pipelines do not perform transformations beyond what is needed for Raw-layer loading; no curated, semantic, or reporting modeling is produced.
preconditions: Curated layer not provisioned for this project.
Expected Result: Raw layer datasets retain source fidelity; no derived/aggregated tables are created by these pipelines; no Power BI semantic model is built by the ingestion project.
steps: Step 1: Inspect pipeline configuration and outputs.
Step 2: Confirm only Landing and Raw outputs exist.
Step 3: Verify no curated/semantic transformations are deployed.

test_name: Non-Moogsoft datasets are excluded from ingestion
test_description: Validate that only Moogsoft-source tables are ingested and no non-Moogsoft data flows into Landing or Raw via these pipelines.
preconditions: Pipeline configuration restricted to Moogsoft sources.
Expected Result: Only Moogsoft tables listed in Section 2.3 are ingested; attempts to add non-Moogsoft sources via misconfiguration are blocked or fail validation.
steps: Step 1: Review ingested dataset list and confirm Moogsoft-only.
Step 2: Attempt to configure a non-Moogsoft source.
Step 3: Verify the addition is rejected or flagged.

test_name: Future PII data detection triggers governance notification
test_description: Validate that if any future PII/PHI data unexpectedly arrives from Moogsoft, the pipeline notifies the project and governance teams and pauses ingestion of affected datasets pending approval.
preconditions: PII detection and notification workflow implemented.
Expected Result: Synthetic PII record triggers detection, governance notification is sent, affected dataset ingestion is paused or quarantined, and resumption requires governance approval.
steps: Step 1: Inject a synthetic PII-like value into a test source field.
Step 2: Run pipeline.
Step 3: Verify detection, alert to governance team, and pipeline pause/quarantine.
Step 4: Confirm explicit approval workflow is required to resume.

test_name: Late-arriving NRT event with older last_state_change handled correctly
test_description: Validate that when an NRT event arrives out of order with a last_state_change older than an existing open version, SCD2 logic does not corrupt the current version chain.
preconditions: SCD2 implementation handles out-of-order events; sample late events available.
Expected Result: Late event is either inserted as a historical version in correct chronological position or rejected per defined policy; current open version remains intact and only one version per key is current.
steps: Step 1: Insert event v1 at T1, then v2 at T2 (T2 > T1).
Step 2: Emit late event v1.5 with last_state_change between T1 and T2.
Step 3: Query target SCD2 table.
Step 4: Verify chronological integrity and single open version.

test_name: Duplicate NRT events with identical last_state_change are deduplicated
test_description: Validate that duplicate NRT events for the same key with the same last_state_change value do not create duplicate SCD2 versions or duplicate rows in the streaming target.
preconditions: SCD2 logic includes idempotency on (key, last_state_change).
Expected Result: Only one row exists per (key, last_state_change); duplicates are ignored or treated as no-op.
steps: Step 1: Emit the same event twice with identical last_state_change.
Step 2: Query the target table.
Step 3: Verify only one row exists for that (key, last_state_change) combination.

test_name: Full fidelity preserved from source to Raw
test_description: Validate that all data loaded into Landing and Raw layers retains full fidelity and accuracy from the source (no truncation, rounding, character encoding loss, or column drops).
preconditions: Source contains a representative sample including special characters, large strings, max numeric values, and nullable columns.
Expected Result: Raw layer rows exactly match source rows for all columns; checksum comparisons reconcile; no character encoding or precision loss.
steps: Step 1: Seed source with diverse representative records.
Step 2: Run ingestion.
Step 3: Compare row-by-row and column-by-column between source and Raw.
Step 4: Verify zero discrepancies.

test_name: Quarantine handling for malformed records
test_description: Validate that invalid or malformed records are quarantined with appropriate logging that enables remediation rather than being silently dropped.
preconditions: Quarantine destination configured.
Expected Result: Malformed records appear in quarantine store with original payload, failure reason, run id, and timestamp; remediation team can replay or correct them.
steps: Step 1: Inject malformed records into source.
Step 2: Run pipeline.
Step 3: Inspect quarantine store for required fields.
Step 4: Validate ability to reprocess after remediation.

test_name: Backfill restart resumes without duplication
test_description: Validate that if the one-time historical backfill is interrupted and restarted, it resumes correctly without producing duplicate rows in the consolidated Until<YYYYMMDD> tables.
preconditions: Backfill pipeline supports restart/checkpointing.
Expected Result: After interruption and restart, the consolidated table contains each source record exactly once; counts reconcile with source totals.
steps: Step 1: Begin backfill.
Step 2: Interrupt mid-run (kill task).
Step 3: Restart backfill.
Step 4: Validate target table has no duplicates and row count matches source.
requirement_mapping: Section 1.1 In Scope - One-Time Historical Backfill; Section 1.6 Availability & Reliability

test_name: Application criticality 'Gold' SLA monitoring
test_description: Validate that because Moogsoft is categorized as 'Gold', monitoring, alerting, and SLA enforcement align with Gold-tier requirements (e.g., 24x7 monitoring, rapid alerting, high-availability targets).
preconditions: Monitoring tier configured to Gold.
Expected Result: Pipeline observability and incident response thresholds meet Gold-tier requirements; alerts are routed to on-call channels.
steps: Step 1: Review monitoring/alerting configuration for Gold-tier SLAs.
Step 2: Simulate failure scenario.
Step 3: Validate alert routing and response timelines meet Gold-tier policy.

test_name: Scale test for future Moogsoft event frequency growth
test_description: Validate that the pipeline accommodates a 2x-5x increase in NRT event frequency to confirm future scalability without breaching latency SLAs.
preconditions: Load generator capable of emitting elevated NRT volumes to Event Hub.
Expected Result: NRT pipeline sustains elevated load with latency remaining ≤60 seconds and no error rate increase beyond baseline.
steps: Step 1: Establish baseline NRT throughput and latency.
Step 2: Increase emission rate to 2x.
Step 3: Increase emission rate to 5x.
Step 4: Measure latency and error rates and confirm SLA compliance.

test_name: Alert and situation archive one-time copy via copy activity
test_description: Validate that alert_archive and situation_archive are ingested via a one-time copy activity during initial load and are subsequently not re-copied by daily or NRT pipelines.
preconditions: Archive tables populated; copy activity defined.
Expected Result: alert_archive and situation_archive are copied once during initial load; no subsequent pipeline executes copy activity against them; their data is part of the Until<YYYYMMDD> tables only.
steps: Step 1: Execute initial-load copy activity for archive tables.
Step 2: Validate the data reflects in consolidated Until tables.
Step 3: Run NRT and daily batch multiple times.
Step 4: Confirm archive copy activity does not re-execute.
requirement_mapping: Section 2.3 Table names (alert_archive/situation_archive - one-time copy activity)

test_name: Open Questions - missing information not addressed
test_description: Document open questions discovered while reviewing the BRD: (1) exact retention period for logs; (2) defined retry counts/backoff for transient failures; (3) PROD Event Hub connection details (left blank in BRD section 2.2); (4) specific operational SLA window length for daily batch completion; (5) policy for handling late-arriving NRT events (insert vs reject); (6) Power BI access role definitions. These should be clarified by stakeholders before final test execution.
preconditions: N/A - documentation gap tracker.
Expected Result: All open questions are addressed by business/technical stakeholders and the requirements/tests are updated accordingly.
steps: Step 1: Circulate open questions list to stakeholders.
Step 2: Capture answers and update BRD.
Step 3: Update or add test cases to cover newly clarified requirements.
